title: [Bug]: Oracle benchmark reports can render non-standard JSON values
summary: Oracle benchmark phase timing objects can carry non-finite values and the JSON report renderer uses default json.dumps behavior, allowing NaN or Infinity in public evidence.
observed: A benchmark phase containing NaN can be represented and rendered as non-standard JSON.
expected: Benchmark timing objects should reject non-finite timing values before report rendering.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that constructs an Oracle benchmark phase with NaN timing data.
impact: Benchmark reports may be uploaded as public issue or release evidence. They should never contain non-standard JSON.
delivery_semantics: No runtime delivery behavior changes. This fix affects benchmark evidence generation only.
security: The fix must preserve benchmark redaction and must not include service locators, credentials, table names, wallet paths, certificates, or payload data.
acceptance: A focused regression test proves non-finite Oracle benchmark phase timings are rejected.
Valid benchmark reports still render as JSON and Markdown.
Existing Oracle benchmark tests still pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, Oracle benchmark tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and testing documentation to mention strict benchmark evidence output.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, regression evidence, verification evidence, and any remaining limitations.
